channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Bengali New Year Procession in Kolkata Faces EC Denial; Organisation Proceeds Regardless
item.description: On April 15, 2026, a Bengali nationalist group, 'Bangla Pokkho', raised concerns after the Election Commission (EC) reportedly refused permission for their 'Poila Baisakh' (Bengali New Year) processio...

item.title: Delhi Court Summons Robert Vadra and Others in Haryana Land Deal Money Laundering Case
item.description: On Wednesday, a Delhi court summoned businessman Robert Vadra, husband of Congress leader Priyanka Gandhi Vadra, along with eight others in connection with a money laundering case related to alleged i...

item.title: Trump indicates potential nearing end of Iran conflict as peace talks may resume in Pakistan
item.description: US President Donald Trump has suggested that the war with Iran may be approaching its conclusion, stating in recent interviews that the conflict is 'close to over'. He hinted that peace talks could re...

item.title: Islamabad Bar Association to discuss sentence suspension pleas of Imaan and Hadi after large lawyer petition
item.description: ISLAMABAD: The Islamabad Bar Association (IBA) has called a meeting of its general body on April 21 following a resolution signed by around 400 lawyers urging an urgent hearing of the sentence suspens...

item.title: Opposition INDIA Bloc Rejects Proposed Delimitation Bill
item.description: On April 15, 2026, All India Congress Committee (AICC) President and Rajya Sabha Leader of Opposition Mallikarjun Kharge stated that the INDIA bloc has concluded discussions on the Delimitation Bill a...

item.title: Nigeria’s Headline Inflation Rises Slightly to 15.38% in March 2026
item.description: Nigeria dey experience small increase for headline inflation as e climb to 15.38% for March 2026, show say e don increase small from 15.06% wey e reach for February. National Bureau of Statistics (NBS...

item.title: About 15 cargo ships cross Strait of Hormuz in early days of U.S. blockade on Iranian ports
item.description: Recent ship‑tracking data indicate that approximately 15 cargo ships have transited the Strait of Hormuz since the United States began enforcing a naval blockade against vessels linked to Iranian port...

item.title: Satellite images show China tightening blockade at Scarborough Shoal amid renewed tensions
item.description: Satellite images from April 10 and 11, 2026, show that China has placed a new floating barrier and several fishing and patrol vessels around the entrance to the Scarborough Shoal, a disputed maritime ...

item.title: Daily Superquiz and Mini Crossword Details for 16 April 2026
item.description: The Sydney Morning Herald and The Age continue to offer engaging daily puzzles for their readers, with the Superquiz and Mini Crossword taking centre stage on Thursday, 16 April 2026. These bite-sized...

item.title: Negros Occidental jeepney drivers cope with rising fuel prices through extra jobs and odd-even schedule
item.description: In Negros Occidental, jeepney drivers are struggling to cope with the rising cost of diesel caused by the ongoing Middle East crisis. Drivers like 47-year-old Rudy Soriano now earn around P200 daily, ...

item.title: High Court invalidates Victoria’s political donation laws over unfair treatment of independents and smaller parties
item.description: The High Court of Australia has struck down Part 12 of the Victorian Electoral Act, which regulated political donations, campaign spending caps, and public funding for parties. The law was found uncon...

item.title: Meghan Markle Highlights Australia Trip with Online Shopping Links
item.description: During a recent trip to Australia, Meghan Markle, Duchess of Sussex, sparked attention online by linking to the outfits she wore during various engagements. Fans quickly noticed and shared details abo...

item.title: JV Ejercito proposes Midwifery Empowerment Act to support Filipino midwives
item.description: Senator JV Ejercito has introduced the 'Midwifery Empowerment Act,' a proposed legislation intended to protect and improve the welfare of midwives in the Philippines. Speaking during the 27th General ...

item.title: Rising Witchcraft-Related Killings Among Elderly in Malawi Highlight Governance Failures
item.description: Malawi is confronting a worsening crisis where elderly citizens are increasingly targeted in witchcraft-related killings. According to the Malawi Network of Older Persons Organisations (Manepo), 11 el...

item.title: Investigation Reveals Fraudulent Practices in Some UK Asylum Applications
item.description: An undercover investigation by the BBC has revealed a network of advisers and claimants engaging in fraudulent asylum practices in the UK. The report describes how some migrants are coached to fabrica...

item.title: Police probe attempted arson at synagogue amid antisemitic attack
item.description: Police are conducting an investigation following an incident at a local synagogue, where an attempt was made to commit arson in what authorities believe to be an antisemitic attack. The attack has rai...

item.title: Understanding ACL Injuries: Causes, Recovery, and Long-Term Impact
item.description: Anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injuries are among the most feared by athletes, but they affect people of all ages, not just professionals. The ACL connects the thigh and shin bones and plays a criti...

item.title: Government announces extra $53 billion for Defence over the next decade to reach 3 per cent of GDP
item.description: The Australian Government will inject an additional $53 billion into Defence spending over the next ten years, as part of an updated National Defence Strategy to be released by Defence Minister Richar...

item.title: Two Men Killed in Datu Paglas Ambush Amid Rising Violence in Maguindanao del Sur
item.description: In a continuing pattern of violence in Maguindanao del Sur, two men riding a motorcycle were ambushed and killed on April 14, 2026, in Barangay Damawato, Datu Paglas. One of the victims, Ibrahim Matuk...

item.title: Alberta Takes Independent Policy Actions Diverging from Federal Standards
item.description: Over the past few years, Alberta has increasingly challenged federal authority by implementing provincial policies that differ from Ottawa’s standards. Notable actions include tightening eligibility f...
